channel.title: Self-Hosting Cooklang and Integrations with Other Tools on Cooklang
channel.description: Recent content in Self-Hosting Cooklang and Integrations with Other Tools on Cooklang

item.title: A Plain Text Recipe Database You Already Know How to Use
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: You don't need Postgres, schemas, or migrations to build a recipe database. Your filesystem already is one — and CookCLI gives you the query layer on top.

item.title: The Desktop App Is Gone. Here's What Replaced It.
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: We replaced the Cooklang desktop app with a lightweight sync agent — a tiny background service that does the same job faster, with less overhead.

item.title: Cooklang Mobile App: Read, Cook, and Shop from Your Phone
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A walkthrough of the Cooklang mobile app for iOS and Android — how to set up your recipe collection, cook from your phone, and generate shopping lists on the go.

item.title: Building a Recipe API with Cooklang
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Skip the third-party recipe APIs. With CookCLI, your plain-text .cook files become a structured JSON backend you own and control.

item.title: Cooklang + Home Assistant: Smart Kitchen Meal Planning & Recipes
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Turn Home Assistant into a smart kitchen hub by integrating CookCLI — display today's recipe, trigger shopping lists, track expiring pantry items, and fire smart timers from your recipes.

item.title: Build a Raspberry Pi Kitchen Display for Your Recipes
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Turn a Raspberry Pi into a dedicated kitchen recipe display. Run CookCLI's web server on the Pi, sync your Cooklang recipes, and browse them from a touchscreen or any device on your network.

item.title: Self-Hosting Your Recipe Collection with Docker and CookCLI
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Set up a self-hosted recipe server in minutes with Docker and CookCLI. Browse recipes from any device, generate shopping lists, and keep your data private — no cloud accounts required.

item.title: Developing file sync library
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Alexey on a quest of solving recipe sync problem for Cooklang apps.
